basics.name: Neeli Shyam Sridhar
basics.summary: Currently employed at Svaya Robotics Pvt. Ltd. Short biography for the left-hand sidebar

work.summary: Motion planning engineer for a dual-arm robotic system.
highlights: Diagnosed and optimized motion planner execution timing, reducing latency to sub-1 ms and improving real-time determinism
Identified and resolved real-time timing bottlenecks through collaboration with the embedded systems team
Modeled motor friction characteristics to enhance torque-based collision detection accuracy in a dual-arm robotic system
Contributed to system architecture migration from a Finite State Machine (FSM) based planner to a Behavior Tree (BT) based framework, improving modularity of the codebase
Implemented sampling-based motion planners (RRT, RRT*, ABIT*) from scratch without MoveIt, directly integrated into the robot's planning stack
Annotated vision datasets using Roboflow to support training of perception models
Collaborated with the Computer Vision team to integrate perception outputs with the motion planning stack
Tested and validated new features and APIs to ensure system reliability and performance

publications.name: Collaborative Payload Transport Using a Four-Quadcopter Swarm with RTK and MQTT-Based Coordination
publications.publisher: 2025 International Conference on Robotics and Mechatronics (ICRM), IEEE
publications.summary: Design and experimental validation of a four-quadcopter swarm system for collaborative payload transport, using a leader-follower architecture with MQTT coordination and RTK GNSS positioning. Successfully transported a 1 kg payload outdoors using PX4 Autopilot.

teaching.institution: IIT Hyderabad, Department of Mechanical and Aerospace Engineering
teaching.description: Conducted tutorials and supported lab sessions covering kinematics, dynamics, motion planning, and robot programming (2023–2025).

teaching.institution: IIT Hyderabad, Department of Mechanical and Aerospace Engineering
teaching.description: Taught hands-on lab sessions on PLCs and DAQ systems, including sensor interfacing, signal conditioning, and real-time control (2023–2025).
